topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an oppressive, relentless sound. Every glance from a passing student felt like a physical weight pressing down on my shoulders. I kept my gaze fixed on the textbook, the dense paragraphs blurring into meaningless ink. My hands, slick with nervous sweat, kept fiddling with the edge of my notebook, a frantic, repetitive motion. When Professor Albright finally called my name, the sound seemed to echo in the cavernous room, amplifying the silence that followed. The accusation hung in the air, sharp and undeniable. I could feel the heat rising in my neck, a flush spreading across my cheeks that felt horribly exposed. I simply nodded, my throat suddenly dry, and walked toward the office, each step feeling measured and agonizingly slow.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, the damning email glowing starkly against the dark background. The words felt like shards of glass scraping against my insides. My stomach clenched into a tight, hard knot, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I reread the accusation three times, the familiar phrases twisting into something venomous. Outside the window, the autumn rain lashed against the glass, mimicking the chaotic churning inside my chest. I wanted to slam my laptop shut, to disappear, but my fingers remained frozen over the keyboard. A low, involuntary sound escaped my lips—a small, choked gasp—and I quickly clamped my mouth shut, forcing my expression into a mask of blank neutrality.

[story 3]

The hallway smelled faintly of old coffee and disinfectant, a scent that usually offered a small comfort, but today it only sharpened the edges of my anxiety. I clutched the strap of my backpack so tightly my knuckles were white. Every casual interaction—a passing classmate, the librarian shelving books—felt like an interrogation. When the department head finally cornered me near the faculty lounge, his voice was level, devoid of warmth, which only made my skin crawl. I looked down at my shoes, focusing intently on the scuff marks, trying desperately to anchor myself to something solid. A tremor ran through my hands, and I managed a strained, almost inaudible reply, wishing the floor would just open up and swallow me whole.
